events.title: US and Iran exchange heavy missile and drone assaults
events.desc: US and Iranian forces engaged in significant missile and drone exchanges, with Iran targeting US facilities across the Gulf and declaring the closure of the Strait of Hormuz. This escalation jeopardizes the interim agreement aimed at reopening the strait and increases concerns over global oil prices.

events.title: Iran attacks US military bases in Bahrain, Kuwait, and Jordan
events.desc: Iran launched attacks targeting U.S. military assets in Bahrain, Kuwait, and Jordan. This action follows renewed fighting and escalates regional tensions, particularly concerning the security of the Strait of Hormuz.

events.title: US launches more strikes on Iran
events.desc: The United States conducted a new wave of strikes targeting Iran, aiming to degrade Tehran's capabilities to threaten shipping in the Strait of Hormuz. This action is part of the ongoing retaliatory exchanges between the two nations.

events.title: US-Iran strikes escalate over Strait of Hormuz, oil prices surge
events.desc: The United States military conducted strikes on Iranian targets for a second day, intensifying the conflict over the Strait of Hormuz. Tehran's announcement of closing the strait and the renewed fighting have caused oil prices to spike significantly.

events.title: Iran announces closure of Strait of Hormuz amid ongoing strikes
events.desc: Following extensive U.S. strikes on its facilities, Iran declared the closure of the vital Strait of Hormuz. This move escalates the conflict and threatens global energy supplies, leading to a sharp increase in oil prices.

events.title: US insists Strait of Hormuz is open amidst ongoing exchanges with Iran
events.desc: Despite Iranian forces declaring the Strait of Hormuz closed and launching attacks on U.S. allies and bases, the U.S. maintains that the vital waterway remains open. The ongoing military exchanges heighten regional instability.

events.title: Lebanon conflict intensifies with Israeli attacks
events.desc: Israel conducted significant attacks in Lebanon, resulting in casualties, including a headteacher and three women travelling in a car. These attacks mark the deadliest incident since the most recent ceasefire, indicating a potential escalation of hostilities.

events.title: Six killed in Gaza Strip by Israeli attacks
events.desc: Israeli attacks in the Gaza Strip resulted in the deaths of at least six people, including a 9-year-old girl, according to Palestinian health officials. Mediators are engaged in discussions to secure a ceasefire.

events.title: Syrian transitional parliament holds first session
events.desc: Syria's new transitional parliament convened for its inaugural session, tasked with drafting a new constitution and establishing democratic foundations following years of conflict. The session marks a significant step in the country's political transition.

events.title: Iraqi Prime Minister to visit Washington for strategic talks
events.desc: The Prime Minister of Iraq is scheduled to visit Washington for discussions aimed at deepening strategic ties. The visit is expected to result in the signing of oil and gas deals, as part of a broader effort to enhance economic cooperation.

events.title: Bomb explosions injure 18 in Damascus
events.desc: Multiple bomb explosions occurred in Damascus, injuring 18 people. The incident happened during the visit of French President Macron, though state television reported that the Syrian president welcomed Macron at the presidential palace.

events.title: Hizballah conflict with Israel remains active under nominal ceasefire
events.desc: Despite an agreement to terminate military operations, the conflict between Hizballah and Israel remains active. This ongoing tension highlights the fragility of the ceasefire and the persistent threat of escalation in the region.
